hero.title:
Welcome to
Speedcubing Belgium
hero.subtitle: Join the fastest growing speedcubing community in Belgium

about.title:
What is
Speedcubing Belgium?
paragraphs: Speedcubing Belgium (SCB) is the official representative organization for speedcubing in Belgium, working in close collaboration with the World Cube Association (WCA).
We organize and oversee official speedcubing competitions throughout Belgium, where cubers of all skill levels compete to solve various twisty puzzles as fast as possible.
Whether you're a beginner who just learned to solve a speedcube or an experienced speedcuber chasing national records, SCB provides a welcoming community and competitive platform for all.

items.title: Organize Competitions
items.description: We organize official WCA competitions across Belgium, ensuring fair play and professional event management.

items.title: Promote Speedcubing
items.description: We work to raise awareness about speedcubing through competitions, social media presence and community outreach.

items.title: Build Community
items.description: We foster a welcoming community where cubers can compete, meet, share techniques and support each other's growth.

joinCta.title: Ready to Join Us?
joinCta.subtitle: Check out our upcoming competitions and become part of the Belgian speedcubing scene!

hero.title: About Us
hero.subtitle: Learn more about Speedcubing Belgium and our mission to promote competitive cubing across the country.

events.title: Speedcubing Arrives in Belgium
events.description: After the establishment of the WCA in 2004, the first Belgian cubers begin attending competitions. Notably, Lars Vandenbergh sets World Records in 4x4, 5x5 and Square-1.

events.title: The First Belgian Competition
events.description: Belgian Open 2006 is the first Belgian competition, bringing together cubers from across the country and connecting the Belgian community to the international speedcubing scene.

events.title: First Growth of Speedcubing
events.description: As of 2012, more competitions are consistently being hosted in Belgium. Lars Vandenbergh (2010-2013) and Geert Bicler (2013-2017) step up as WCA Delegates, giving more opportunity for Belgian speedcubers to compete.

events.title: Establishment of Speedcubing Belgium
events.description: In 2018 Manu Vereecken is appointed as a WCA Delegate. He single-handedly transformed the Belgian speedcubing scene, running competitions more efficiently. Together with other volunteers, Speedcubing Belgium is founded on 27 January 2019 as a central platform for speedcubing in our country. The speedcubing scene starts growing more rapidly.

events.title: Post-Pandemic Expansion
events.description: The COVID-19 pandemic temporarily paused competitions, with the first Belgian competition being held again in November 2021. In 2022, Aurelien Marion and Bruno Vervoort join Manu as delegates. After the pandemic, interest in speedcubing surged with competitions filling up within minutes. The first Belgian National Championship is held in 2022.

events.title: A New Era of Large Competitions
events.description: With more people and more organisers, there are more competitions for more people. In 2024, over 1400 people attended 13 Belgian competitions with more National Records being broken than ever before.

events.title: Speedcubing Belgium
events.description: Belgium currently counts 6 WCA Delegates, having hosted over 100 competitions throughout the years. With more people there are more organizers and more competitions in new locations. Belgian Nationals 2025 is Belgium’s biggest ever competition with 192 competitors.

mission.title: Promoting Speedcubing in Belgium
mission.description: To promote and develop speedcubing in Belgium by organizing high-quality competitions, fostering a vibrant community, and supporting cubers of all levels in their pursuit of excellence.

getInvolved.title: Get Involved
getInvolved.subtitle: Want to contribute to the Belgian speedcubing community? There are many ways to get involved!

hero.title: Upcoming Competitions
hero.subtitle: Join us at official WCA-sanctioned speedcubing competitions across Belgium. All skill levels welcome!

upcoming.title: Upcoming Competitions
upcoming.empty: No upcoming competitions at the moment. Check back soon!

firstTime.title: First Time
firstTime.subtitle: Don't worry! Our competitions are beginner-friendly. Here's what you need to know:
tips: You must be able to solve a speedcube (any speed is fine!)
Registration is done through the World Cube Association website
Bring your own cubes — we recommend having at least one backup
Competitors help judge and scramble for each other — it's part of the experience!

hero.title: National Records
hero.subtitle: Celebrating the fastest times achieved by Belgian speedcubers in official WCA competitions.

info.title: About National Records
info.description: National records are officially recognized by the World Cube Association (WCA) and must be set at official WCA competitions held in Belgium.
facts: Single: The fastest single solve in an event
Average: The average of 3 solves (removing best and worst of the 5 solves)
All times are verified by official WCA delegates and judges

error.failload: Failed to load records. Please try again later.

hero.title: Delegates
hero.subtitle: Meet the WCA delegates who ensure the quality and fairness of speedcubing competitions in Belgium.

info.title: What is a WCA Delegate?
info.description: WCA Delegates are officials appointed by the World Cube Association to oversee competitions and ensure they follow WCA regulations. They are responsible for:
responsibilities: Verifying that competitions meet all WCA standards and regulations
Resolving incidents and ensuring fair play during competitions
Approving and uploading official results to the WCA database
Training judges, scramblers, and organizers on proper procedures
Supporting competition organizers with planning and logistics

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
paragraphs: Speedcubing Belgium (
) is the official representative organisation for speedcubing in Belgium. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect information when you visit our website (speedcubingbelgium.be).
We are committed to protecting your privacy and handling any personal data we obtain with care and respect.

sections.heading: 2. Data Controller
paragraphs: The data controller responsible for this website is Speedcubing Belgium, a non-profit organisation registered in Belgium. You can contact us at info@speedcubingbelgium.be.

sections.heading: 3. Cookies We Use
paragraphs: We use a minimal number of cookies to operate this website.
Functional cookies (always set): preferred_lang stores your chosen language (en / fr / nl) and expires after 1 year. cookie_consent stores your consent choice so we do not ask again on every visit.

Analytics cookies (optional — only set if you accept all): Google Analytics (_ga, _ga_*) collects anonymised data about which pages are visited and how visitors navigate the site. These cookies are only set if you click
in the cookie banner. No personal data is sold or shared with advertisers.
We do not use advertising cookies or third-party tracking cookies.

sections.heading: 4. How We Use Data
paragraphs: Functional cookie data is used solely to remember your language preference and consent choice, ensuring the website works correctly for you.
Analytics data (if consented) is used only to improve the website experience. This data is aggregated and anonymised, and we have enabled IP anonymisation in Google Analytics.

sections.heading: 5. Third Parties
paragraphs: If you accept analytics cookies, anonymised usage data is processed by Google LLC via Google Analytics. Google may transfer this data to servers in the United States under standard contractual clauses.
We do not sell your data or share it with any other third parties.

sections.heading: 6. Your Rights
paragraphs: Under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the right to access, rectify, or request erasure of any personal data we hold about you. You can withdraw your analytics consent at any time by clearing your browser cookies or declining in the cookie banner.
For any data-related requests, please contact us at info@speedcubingbelgium.be.

sections.heading: 7. Changes to This Policy
paragraphs: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. The date at the top of this page reflects the most recent revision. Continued use of the website after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
paragraphs: These Terms & Conditions govern your use of the Speedcubing Belgium website (speedcubingbelgium.be), operated by Speedcubing Belgium, a non-profit organisation registered in Belgium. By accessing or using the website, you agree to be bound by these terms.

sections.heading: 2. Use of the Website
paragraphs: You may use this website for lawful, personal, and non-commercial purposes only. You must not misuse, interfere with, or attempt to gain unauthorised access to any part of the website or its underlying systems.
We reserve the right to suspend or terminate access to the website at any time without notice.

sections.heading: 3. Intellectual Property
paragraphs: All content on this website — including text, images, logos, and source code — is the property of Speedcubing Belgium unless otherwise stated. You may not reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works without our prior written consent.
The Speedcubing Belgium name and logo are trademarks of the organisation. The Rubik's Cube and related puzzle brands are trademarks of their respective owners.

sections.heading: 4. Third-Party Links
paragraphs: This website contains links to third-party websites, including the World Cube Association (worldcubeassociation.org). These links are provided for convenience only. We have no control over those sites and accept no responsibility for their content, privacy practices, or availability.

sections.heading: 5. Limitation of Liability
paragraphs: We strive to keep the information on this website accurate and up to date, but we make no warranties regarding the completeness, accuracy, or reliability of any content.

To the fullest extent permitted by Belgian law, Speedcubing Belgium is not liable for any direct, indirect, or consequential loss or damage arising from your use of, or inability to use, this website.

sections.heading: 6. Governing Law
paragraphs: These Terms & Conditions are governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law. Any disputes arising from the use of this website shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Belgium.

sections.heading: 7. Contact
paragraphs: If you have any questions about these Terms & Conditions, please contact us at info@speedcubingbelgium.be.

about.heading: About SCB
about.description: Speedcubing Belgium is the official representative organization for competitive speedcube solving in Belgium, affiliated with the World Cube Association (WCA).
